name: content
description: Generic prose slide — heading plus paragraphs or a short list.
purpose: The catch-all for explanatory content that doesn't fit a more structured layout. Resist using it when a more specific component (cards-grid, stats, compare-prose) would shape the content better.

body.description: Paragraphs or a short bullet list under the heading. Keep under ~40 words.

skeleton:
<!-- _class: content -->

## Slide heading.

The explanatory paragraph that develops the heading goes here. Keep the slide under forty words.

- Optional supporting point one.
- Optional supporting point two.
sample:
<!-- _class: content -->

## The catch-all prose slide.

Use `content` when no more specific component fits. A heading, a paragraph, optionally a short bullet list. Keep the slide under forty words.

- For lists of items, prefer `list`, `cards-grid`, or `cards-stack`.
- For comparisons, prefer `compare-prose` or `compare-table`.
- For single metrics, prefer `big-number` or `stats`.

whenToUse.title: Explanatory prose that doesn't shape
whenToUse.body: A paragraph that develops one idea. No comparisons to spell out, no inventory to grid, no metric to highlight — just prose with a heading. The catch-all when shape would be forced.

whenToUse.title: Under forty words
whenToUse.body: Content slides earn their place when they're brief. Past 40 words the slide becomes a wall of text and the audience stops reading. Trim or split into two slides.

whenToUse.title: Optional short bullet list
whenToUse.body: If the paragraph wants two or three loose qualifications, a bullet list below the prose is fine. For more than that, the content is really structured — move to a `list` or `cards-stack` slide.

antiPatterns.title: Forced shape into prose
antiPatterns.body: If the content is a comparison, use compare-prose. If it's a list of options, use cards-grid. If it's a sequence, use list-steps. Reaching for content when shape exists wastes the slide.

antiPatterns.title: Wall of text
antiPatterns.body: More than 40 words and the audience tunes out. The layout doesn't fight back — it'll happily render a 200-word paragraph that nobody reads. Split or trim.

antiPatterns.title: Multiple headings
antiPatterns.body: Content carries one heading and one idea. Two h2s on one slide reads as two slides crammed together. Split into two content slides or use a structured layout.

related.name: quote
related.when: the prose IS a quote — let the quotation chrome carry it

related.name: big-number
related.when: the prose IS a metric — let the number carry it

related.name: cards-grid
related.when: the prose IS a parallel list of items

related.name: compare-prose
related.when: the prose IS a two-way comparison

related.name: list-steps
related.when: the prose IS an ordered sequence
